problem_description: The client wants a scalable system with a strict security to manage thousands of scanned documents that were difficult to search and organize. They also required a development model that worked on both on-premise and in cloud environemnts

non_functional: Remain responsive under heavy document upload workloads
Designed for future machine learning integration
Modular and extensible architecture for future features

solution: I designed a document ingestion pipeline where uploads are stored immediately while OCR processing runs asynchronously. Extracted text is indexed for search, and storage is abstracted behind an S3-compatible interface to support different deployment environments.
design_principle: Because the system was originally intended for government use, reliability and security were primary concerns. I designed the document ingestion pipeline to prioritize durability over immediate processing. Documents should never be lost due to temporary failures, uploads should not block users, and recoverable errors should be handled automatically without manual intervention.

architecture.diagram_description: I Designed this upload pipeline with the assumption that failures will be unavoidable, therefore it is built to be recoverable from  them.

technical_decisions.title: Nextjs as Frontend
technical_decisions.challenge: The system requires to be responsive and modern
technical_decisions.decision: Implemented Nextjs as a frontend, a React based framework with robust production ready ecosystem that features modern and responsive UI

technical_decisions.title: Django as Backend
technical_decisions.challenge: The system requires to be ready for machine learning integration
technical_decisions.decision: Implemented Django as backend due to being based on python with robust production ready ecosystem.
outcome: Robust libraries for faster and robust development

technical_decisions.title: File indexing and Semantic search
technical_decisions.challenge: The system requires file indexing and semantic search
technical_decisions.decision: Implemented postgres as database with pgvector support and OCR text extraction

technical_decisions.title: Object Storage
technical_decisions.challenge: Deploying the system between on-premise and cloud will affect the upload pipeline and will require refactoring of code.
technical_decisions.decision: Integrated an s3 compatible Object Storage to seamlessly deploy the system either on-premise or cloud with minimal configuration
outcome: Minimal Configuration when deploying on-premise or as a cloud service

technical_decisions.title: Direct-to-Object-Storage Uploads
technical_decisions.challenge: Uploading large documents through the backend would consume API worker resources and increase response times.
technical_decisions.decision: Implemented direct browser uploads using pre-signed URLs so files are uploaded directly to MinIO. The backend only generates the upload URL and records metadata after the upload completes.

technical_decisions.title: Asynchronous Processing
technical_decisions.challenge: OCR and document indexing are CPU-intensive operations that can take several seconds or longer.
technical_decisions.decision: These tasks are executed by Celery workers after the upload has completed.
outcome: Users can continue browsing the application
Long-running work doesn't block HTTP requests
Worker capacity can be scaled independently

technical_decisions.title: Failure Recovery
technical_decisions.challenge: The system was originally intended for government use, where document loss and service interruptions are unacceptable. Network failures, worker crashes, or corrupted files should not prevent the application from continuing to operate or compromise uploaded documents.
technical_decisions.decision: Designed the upload pipeline around the principle that failures are inevitable. Documents are uploaded and persisted to object storage before any processing begins. Once the upload is confirmed, the backend creates a processing job for background workers. OCR, indexing, and other long-running tasks execute independently of the request lifecycle, allowing failures to be retried without affecting user-facing operations.
outcome: Upload failures do not crash the backend
Users can continue using the application while documents are processed
The ingestion pipeline is resilient to temporary service interruptions

hero.project_name: Web-based RFID Attendance Monitoring system with Facial Verification

problem_description: The school uses biometrics as verification for attendance but still manually writing when logging attendance.

non_functional: Remain responsive under heavy document upload workloads
Designed for future machine learning integration
Modular and extensible architecture for future features

solution: I built an Attendance Monitoring System that automates the logging by simply tapping the RFID and Verification through facial recognition to prevent proxy attendance and unauthorized check ins.
design_principle: Because the system was needs to be deployed fast, secure and reliable. I use monolithic architecture and primarily use production ready frameworks with rich eco-system

architecture.diagram_description: The process of extracting the embedding from the face.
The process of face detection and verification .
